      Data Collection Program U.S. Department of Labor Related Sites

The O*NET® Content Model

The Content Model is the foundation of O*NET. It organizes the most important information about jobs and workers into a clear, structured system.

Content Model

Worker

About the person

Worker Characteristics

Who you are and how you work
  • Career Interests
    • Career Types
    • Specific Areas
  • Abilities
  • Work Styles

Worker Requirements

What you need to know and be able to do
  • Skills
    • Software
    • Essential
    • Transferable
  • Knowledge
  • Education

Experience Requirements

Work experience and credentials you need
  • Experience and Training
  • Licensing

Job

About the work

Occupational Requirements

Work commonly needed for jobs
  • Work Activities
    • General
    • Intermediate
    • Detailed
  • Work Context

Occupation-Specific Info

Details about a specific job
  • Occupation Title
  • Description
  • Job Titles
  • Tasks

Market

About salary and job demand

Workforce Characteristics

How the current and future job markets look
  • Labor Market Information
  • Occupational Outlook

The Content Model is based on research about jobs and how people work. It describes what’s involved in various jobs, as well as the skills and qualities workers need.

It includes information that can be used across many jobs and industries, as well as details specific to particular occupations. This information is organized into six main areas to help you explore key aspects of jobs and workers.

To download a full list of Content Model variables, visit the Current data files page. You can also find detailed data collected for each occupation. In the outline below, variables marked with are collected by O*NET and have detailed data available.

Worker Characteristics

Personal traits and abilities that affect how people learn and perform at work.

  • Folder (closedopen) Abilities52 variables 92,976 ratings
    Natural talents and capabilities that affect how well someone can perform work.
    • Folder (closedopen)Cognitive Abilities
      Abilities that influence the acquisition and application of knowledge in problem solving
      • Folder (closedopen)Verbal Abilities
        Abilities that influence the acquisition and application of verbal information in problem solving
        • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborOral Comprehension
          The ability to listen to and understand information and ideas presented through spoken words and sentences.
        • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborWritten Comprehension
          The ability to read and understand information and ideas presented in writing.
        • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborOral Expression
          The ability to communicate information and ideas in speaking so others will understand.
        • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborWritten Expression
          The ability to communicate information and ideas in writing so others will understand.
      • Folder (closedopen)Idea Generation and Reasoning Abilities
        Abilities that influence the application and manipulation of information in problem solving
        • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborFluency of Ideas
          The ability to come up with a number of ideas about a topic (the number of ideas is important, not their quality, correctness, or creativity).
        • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborOriginality
          The ability to come up with unusual or clever ideas about a given topic or situation, or to develop creative ways to solve a problem.
        • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborProblem Sensitivity
          The ability to tell when something is wrong or is likely to go wrong. It does not involve solving the problem, only recognizing that there is a problem.
        • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborDeductive Reasoning
          The ability to apply general rules to specific problems to produce answers that make sense.
        • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborInductive Reasoning
          The ability to combine pieces of information to form general rules or conclusions (includes finding a relationship among seemingly unrelated events).
        • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborInformation Ordering
          The ability to arrange things or actions in a certain order or pattern according to a specific rule or set of rules (e.g., patterns of numbers, letters, words, pictures, mathematical operations).
        • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborCategory Flexibility
          The ability to generate or use different sets of rules for combining or grouping things in different ways.
      • Folder (closedopen)Quantitative Abilities
        Abilities that influence the solution of problems involving mathematical relationships
        • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborMathematical Reasoning
          The ability to choose the right mathematical methods or formulas to solve a problem.
        • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborNumber Facility
          The ability to add, subtract, multiply, or divide quickly and correctly.
      • Folder (closedopen)Memory
        Abilities related to the recall of available information
        • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborMemorization
          The ability to remember information such as words, numbers, pictures, and procedures.
      • Folder (closedopen)Perceptual Abilities
        Abilities related to the acquisition and organization of visual information
        • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborSpeed of Closure
          The ability to quickly make sense of, combine, and organize information into meaningful patterns.
        • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborFlexibility of Closure
          The ability to identify or detect a known pattern (a figure, object, word, or sound) that is hidden in other distracting material.
        • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborPerceptual Speed
          The ability to quickly and accurately compare similarities and differences among sets of letters, numbers, objects, pictures, or patterns. The things to be compared may be presented at the same time or one after the other. This ability also includes comparing a presented object with a remembered object.
      • Folder (closedopen)Spatial Abilities
        Abilities related to the manipulation and organization of spatial information
        • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborSpatial Orientation
          The ability to know your location in relation to the environment or to know where other objects are in relation to you.
        • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborVisualization
          The ability to imagine how something will look after it is moved around or when its parts are moved or rearranged.
      • Folder (closedopen)Attentiveness
        Abilities related to application of attention
        • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborSelective Attention
          The ability to concentrate on a task over a period of time without being distracted.
        • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborTime Sharing
          The ability to shift back and forth between two or more activities or sources of information (such as speech, sounds, touch, or other sources).
    • Folder (closedopen)Psychomotor Abilities
      Abilities that influence the capacity to manipulate and control objects
      • Folder (closedopen)Fine Manipulative Abilities
        Abilities related to the manipulation of objects
        • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborArm-Hand Steadiness
          The ability to keep your hand and arm steady while moving your arm or while holding your arm and hand in one position.
        • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborManual Dexterity
          The ability to quickly move your hand, your hand together with your arm, or your two hands to grasp, manipulate, or assemble objects.
        • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborFinger Dexterity
          The ability to make precisely coordinated movements of the fingers of one or both hands to grasp, manipulate, or assemble very small objects.
      • Folder (closedopen)Control Movement Abilities
        Abilities related to the control and manipulation of objects in time and space
        • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborControl Precision
          The ability to quickly and repeatedly adjust the controls of a machine or a vehicle to exact positions.
        • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborMultilimb Coordination
          The ability to coordinate two or more limbs (for example, two arms, two legs, or one leg and one arm) while sitting, standing, or lying down. It does not involve performing the activities while the whole body is in motion.
        • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborResponse Orientation
          The ability to choose quickly between two or more movements in response to two or more different signals (lights, sounds, pictures). It includes the speed with which the correct response is started with the hand, foot, or other body part.
        • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborRate Control
          The ability to time your movements or the movement of a piece of equipment in anticipation of changes in the speed and/or direction of a moving object or scene.
      • Folder (closedopen)Reaction Time and Speed Abilities
        Abilities related to speed of manipulation of objects
        • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborReaction Time
          The ability to quickly respond (with the hand, finger, or foot) to a signal (sound, light, picture) when it appears.
        • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborWrist-Finger Speed
          The ability to make fast, simple, repeated movements of the fingers, hands, and wrists.
        • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborSpeed of Limb Movement
          The ability to quickly move the arms and legs.
    • Folder (closedopen)Physical Abilities
      Abilities that influence strength, endurance, flexibility, balance and coordination
      • Folder (closedopen)Physical Strength Abilities
        Abilities related to the capacity to exert force
        • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborStatic Strength
          The ability to exert maximum muscle force to lift, push, pull, or carry objects.
        • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborExplosive Strength
          The ability to use short bursts of muscle force to propel oneself (as in jumping or sprinting), or to throw an object.
        • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborDynamic Strength
          The ability to exert muscle force repeatedly or continuously over time. This involves muscular endurance and resistance to muscle fatigue.
        • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborTrunk Strength
          The ability to use your abdominal and lower back muscles to support part of the body repeatedly or continuously over time without "giving out" or fatiguing.
      • Folder (closedopen)Endurance
        The ability to exert oneself physically over long periods without getting out of breath
        • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborStamina
          The ability to exert yourself physically over long periods of time without getting winded or out of breath.
      • Folder (closedopen)Flexibility, Balance, and Coordination
        Abilities related to the control of gross body movements
        • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborExtent Flexibility
          The ability to bend, stretch, twist, or reach with your body, arms, and/or legs.
        • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborDynamic Flexibility
          The ability to quickly and repeatedly bend, stretch, twist, or reach out with your body, arms, and/or legs.
        • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborGross Body Coordination
          The ability to coordinate the movement of your arms, legs, and torso together when the whole body is in motion.
        • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborGross Body Equilibrium
          The ability to keep or regain your body balance or stay upright when in an unstable position.
    • Folder (closedopen)Sensory Abilities
      Abilities that influence visual, auditory and speech perception
      • Folder (closedopen)Visual Abilities
        Abilities related to visual sensory input
        • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborNear Vision
          The ability to see details at close range (within a few feet of the observer).
        • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborFar Vision
          The ability to see details at a distance.
        • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborVisual Color Discrimination
          The ability to match or detect differences between colors, including shades of color and brightness.
        • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborNight Vision
          The ability to see under low-light conditions.
        • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborPeripheral Vision
          The ability to see objects or movement of objects to one's side when the eyes are looking ahead.
        • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborDepth Perception
          The ability to judge which of several objects is closer or farther away from you, or to judge the distance between you and an object.
        • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborGlare Sensitivity
          The ability to see objects in the presence of a glare or bright lighting.
      • Folder (closedopen)Auditory and Speech Abilities
        Abilities related to auditory and oral input
        • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborHearing Sensitivity
          The ability to detect or tell the differences between sounds that vary in pitch and loudness.
        • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborAuditory Attention
          The ability to focus on a single source of sound in the presence of other distracting sounds.
        • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborSound Localization
          The ability to tell the direction from which a sound originated.
        • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborSpeech Recognition
          The ability to identify and understand the speech of another person.
        • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborSpeech Clarity
          The ability to speak clearly so others can understand you.
  • Folder (closedopen) Career Interest Types9 variables 8,307 ratings
    Broad types of work you enjoy.
    • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborRealistic
      Work involves designing, building, or repairing of equipment, materials, or structures, engaging in physical activity, or working outdoors. Realistic occupations are often associated with engineering, mechanics and electronics, construction, woodworking, transportation, machine operation, agriculture, animal services, physical or manual labor, athletics, or protective services.
    • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborInvestigative
      Work involves studying and researching non-living objects, living organisms, disease or other forms of impairment, or human behavior. Investigative occupations are often associated with physical, life, medical, or social sciences, and can be found in the fields of humanities, mathematics/statistics, information technology, or health care service.
    • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborArtistic
      Work involves creating original visual artwork, performances, written works, food, or music for a variety of media, or applying artistic principles to the design of various objects and materials. Artistic occupations are often associated with visual arts, applied arts and design, performing arts, music, creative writing, media, or culinary art.
    • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborSocial
      Work involves helping, teaching, advising, assisting, or providing service to others. Social occupations are often associated with social, health care, personal service, teaching/education, or religious activities.
    • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborEnterprising
      Work involves managing, negotiating, marketing, or selling, typically in a business setting, or leading or advising people in political and legal situations. Enterprising occupations are often associated with business initiatives, sales, marketing/advertising, finance, management/administration, professional advising, public speaking, politics, or law.
    • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborConventional
      Work involves following procedures and regulations to organize information or data, typically in a business setting. Conventional occupations are often associated with office work, accounting, mathematics/statistics, information technology, finance, or human resources.
  • Folder (closedopen) Specific Interest Areas41 variables 73,062 ratings
    Work activities you enjoy.
    • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborMechanics/Electronics
      Work involves maintaining and repairing of machinery, such as automotive engines, electronic equipment, computers, and communication systems.
    • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborConstruction/Woodwork
      Work involves constructing, installing, or repairing structures and fixtures made of wood.
    • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborTransportation/Machine Operation
      Work involves driving vehicles, such as vans, trucks, trains, and buses to transport people or materials. Work can also involve operating heavy equipment, such as cranes, forklifts, bulldozers, and backhoes to move materials or earth.
    • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborPhysical/Manual Labor
      Work involves manually unloading and moving freight, stock, luggage, or other materials, or performing other physical labor.
    • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborProtective Service
      Work involves maintaining order and protecting life and property by conducting investigations and enforcing laws and regulations to prevent or solve crimes.
    • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborAgriculture
      Work involves planting, cultivating, and harvesting crops, plants, or trees, or raising farm animals. Work can also involve preparing plant and animal products for distribution to consumers, or protecting natural resources to maximize their use in farming.
    • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborNature/Outdoors
      Work involves planning, restoring, and conserving natural areas and wildlife habitats.
    • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborAnimal Service
      Work involves feeding, grooming, exercising, or otherwise providing care to promote and maintain the well-being of pets and other animals in settings such as kennels, animal shelters, and zoos.
    • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborAthletics
      Work involves training, practicing, or competing in athletic events, or supporting those participating in athletics through coaching, training, or officiating.
    • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborEngineering
      Work involves applying science and technology to the design, building, testing, and use of electrical and electronic components, mechanical devices and machines, automotive, marine, and aerospace equipment and vehicles, materials, or structures.
    • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborPhysical Science
      Work involves studying and researching non-living objects, materials, and phenomena through scientific disciplines such as physics, chemistry, astronomy, meteorology, or geology.
    • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborLife Science
      Work involves studying and researching living organisms through scientific disciplines such as biology, botany, zoology, microbiology, physiology, biochemistry, or zoology.
    • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborMedical Science
      Work involves studying and researching the causes, nature, effects, and treatments of disease or other forms of impairment or illness.
    • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborSocial Science
      Work involves studying and researching human behavior, society, politics, and social relationships through scientific disciplines such as psychology, sociology, economics, or political science.
    • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborHumanities
      Work involves studying and researching aspects of human history, culture, and language through disciplines such as history, philosophy, the language arts, or area/ethnic studies.
    • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborMathematics/Statistics
      Work involves developing and applying mathematics and statistical theory and methods.
    • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborInformation Technology
      Work involves designing, developing, testing, and maintaining computer software, hardware, networks, and systems.
    • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborVisual Arts
      Work involves creating original visual artwork for a variety of media and purposes using various techniques and software.
    • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborApplied Arts and Design
      Work involves applying artistic principles to the design and creation of various objects and materials.
    • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborPerforming Arts
      Work involves acting as a performer in dance, comedy, or dramatic productions, choreographing of dance performances, or producing of live or recorded entertainment.
    • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborMusic
      Work involves conducting, composing, or playing music or singing.
    • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborCreative Writing
      Work involves developing creative written works, such as novels, scripts, song lyrics, poetry, stories, or advertisements.
    • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborMedia
      Work involves producing, developing, writing, directing, or presenting information through print, radio, television, or digital media.
    • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborCulinary Art
      Work involves preparing, cooking, and presenting food in a distinctive style usually associated with a specific culture or region.
    • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborTeaching/Education
      Work involves teaching through lectures, discussions, and demonstrations in one or more subjects.
    • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborSocial Service
      Work involves providing social services and assistance to improve the well-being and social and psychological functioning of children and adults.
    • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborHealth Care Service
      Work involves providing health care services to individuals to include the diagnosis, prevention, or treatment of diseases and other forms of illness or impairment.
    • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborReligious Activities
      Work involves conducting religious worship, leading a church or other religious group, and offering moral guidance associated with beliefs and practices of a religious faith.
    • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborPersonal Service
      Work involves providing personal services to individuals in need of non-medical assistance such as helping arrange or plan for travel, meals, or events.
    • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborProfessional Advising
      Work involves providing expert advice, coaching, and counseling on topics such as careers, educational opportunities, and educational and professional development.
    • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborBusiness Initiatives
      Work involves creating strategic plans or taking actions to expand an organization's business or market share.
    • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborSales
      Work involves selling goods or services.
    • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborMarketing/Advertising
      Work involves promoting or creating a public image for products, services, or organizations to attract interest, engagement, or sales.
    • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborFinance
      Work involves directing and managing financial activities, such as planning and investments for an individual or organization to generate income.
    • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborAccounting
      Work involves examining, analyzing, and interpreting accounting records for financial statements, or maintaining, auditing, or evaluating records and accounts.
    • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborHuman Resources
      Work involves recruiting, selecting, and training job applicants, maintaining employee personnel records, administering compensation and employee-benefit programs, and conducting employee performance reviews.
    • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborOffice Work
      Work involves clerical and administrative tasks for an organization such as answering phones, bookkeeping, updating records, scheduling appointments or meetings, and editing of documents and files.
    • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborManagement/Administration
      Work involves planning, directing, or coordinating the daily operations of an organization, and overseeing personnel, business, or administrative services.
    • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborPublic Speaking
      Work involves speaking before an audience to educate, entertain, or influence the listeners.
    • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborPolitics
      Work involves engaging in political activities to influence government policy.
    • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborLaw
      Work involves studying and applying legal knowledge.
  • Folder (closedopen) Work Styles21 variables 37,422 ratings
    Personality tendencies exhibited at work, which can affect how well someone performs a job.
    • Folder (closedopen)Proactive and Growth Oriented
      A tendency to proactively pursue excellence through setting high standards, engaging in continuous learning, and persisting and adapting in the face of challenges and uncertainty at work.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborInnovation
        A tendency to be inventive, to be imaginative, and to adopt new perspectives on ways to accomplish work.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborAchievement Orientation
        A tendency to establish and maintain personally challenging work-related goals, set high work-related standards, and exert high effort toward meeting those goals and standards.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborIntellectual Curiosity
        A tendency to seek out and acquire new work-related knowledge and obtain a deep understanding of work-related subjects.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborTolerance for Ambiguity
        A tendency to be comfortable with ambiguity and uncertainty at work.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborInitiative
        A tendency to be proactive and take on extra responsibilities and tasks that may fall outside of one's required work role.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborAdaptability
        A tendency to be open to and comfortable with change, new experiences, or ideas at work.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborSelf-Confidence
        A tendency to believe in one's work-related capabilities and ability to control one's work-related outcomes.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborPerseverance
        A tendency to exhibit determination and resolve to perform or complete tasks in the face of difficult circumstances or obstacles at work.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborLeadership Orientation
        A tendency to lead, take charge, offer opinions, and provide direction at work.
    • Folder (closedopen)Interpersonally Oriented
      A tendency to engage with others in a positive, supportive, selfless, and considerate manner at work.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborHumility
        A tendency to be modest and humble when interacting with others at work.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborSincerity
        A tendency to be genuine and sincere in interactions with others at work, without concern for personal gain or self-interest.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborEmpathy
        A tendency to show concern for others and be sensitive to others' needs and feelings at work.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborCooperation
        A tendency to be pleasant, helpful, and willing to assist others at work.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborOptimism
        A tendency to exhibit a positive attitude and positive emotions at work, even under difficult circumstances.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborSocial Orientation
        A tendency to seek out, enjoy, and be energized by social interaction at work.
    • Folder (closedopen)Conscientious and Rule Oriented
      A tendency to approach work with careful attention to quality, reliability, and ethical standards while maintaining organized and thorough work practices.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborCautiousness
        A tendency to be careful, deliberate, and risk-avoidant when making work-related decisions or doing work.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborAttention to Detail
        A tendency to be detail-oriented, organized, and thorough in completing work.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborDependability
        A tendency to be reliable, responsible, and consistent in meeting work-related obligations.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborIntegrity
        A tendency to be honest and ethical at work.
    • Folder (closedopen)Emotionally Resilient
      A tendency to maintain emotional stability and cope effectively in the face of adversity, criticism, or high-pressure situations at work.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborStress Tolerance
        A tendency to cope and function effectively in stressful situations at work.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborSelf-Control
        A tendency to remain calm and composed and to manage emotions effectively in response to criticism or difficult situations at work.

Worker Requirements

Skills and knowledge people need for work, developed through education and experience.

  • Folder (closedopen) Software Skills8,753 workplace examples 31,821 linkages
    Using specific programs and applications for work tasks.
    • Folder (closedopen)Business function specific software
      Software to manage business operations, including project management, sales, or logistics.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborHelpdesk or call center software12 workplace examples
        Software to enable help desk or call center operations.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborProcurement software15 workplace examples
        Software to manage contracts or agreements with suppliers, negotiate conditions, or buy products or services.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborHuman resources software239 workplace examples
        Software to manage employee evaluations, track wages or absenteeism, maintain safety records, or manage hours or time off.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborMaterials requirements planning logistics and supply chain software171 workplace examples
        Software to manage, monitor, or optimize the flow of goods or services from an origin point to a destination.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborProject management software207 workplace examples
        Software to plan, organize, staff, or control the completion of a product or set of tasks.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborInventory management software83 workplace examples
        Software to maintain the inventory of businesses including restaurants or retail shops.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborBar coding software2 workplace examples
        Software to interpret, print, or create bar codes for identifying commercial products or parts.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborLabel making software11 workplace examples
        Software to create or print custom labels with text or graphics.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborExpert system software48 workplace examples
        Software to solve complex problems by applying rules to a set of known facts.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborLicense management software2 workplace examples
        Software to manage or monitor the use of software with license restrictions. May track the number of copies in use or computer hardware configurations.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborOffice suite software14 workplace examples
        Software to provide word processing, spreadsheet, e-mail, presentations, or data management capabilities.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborSales and marketing software11 workplace examples
        Software to manage, plan, analyze, or automate marketing campaigns, content creation, or social media interactions.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborMailing and shipping software2 workplace examples
        Software to print postage stamps or shipping labels, or manage pickup or delivery of shipments.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborAudit software1 workplace example
        Software to manage internal company audits or controls. May analyze or extract data, detect forgeries, or provide ongoing monitoring.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborProcedure management software3 workplace examples
        Software to create, manage, or monitor various work flow procedures, to increase efficiency in time or costs.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborProcess mapping and design software10 workplace examples
        Software to create visual representations of business work flows, also called flow charts or workflow diagrams.
    • Folder (closedopen)Finance accounting and enterprise resource planning ERP software
      Software to manage financial activities, including accounts payable, accounts receivable, billing, cost analysis, tax preparation, or financial reporting.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborAccounting software141 workplace examples
        Software to enable business functions relating to accounting, such as recording transactions or managing budgets.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborEnterprise resource planning ERP software266 workplace examples
        Software to integrate, monitor, or analyze data from multiple business operations within a unified system.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborTax preparation software37 workplace examples
        Software to manage or prepare financial information for tax reporting purposes.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborFinancial analysis software356 workplace examples
        Software to gather information or conduct financial analysis.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborTime accounting software67 workplace examples
        Software to submit or manage time accounting for human resources.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborBilling and invoicing software29 workplace examples
        Software to organize or automate billing, invoicing, or payment collection processes.
    • Folder (closedopen)Computer game or entertainment software
      Software to play games or provide entertainment on a computer.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborAction games4 workplace examples
        Software games that focus on action or dexterity skills.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborMusic or sound editing software102 workplace examples
        Software to record or convert analog sounds to a digital format, or to apply effects such as fades, noise filtering, or volume adjustment.
    • Folder (closedopen)Content authoring and editing software
      Software to create or edit digital content.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborPattern design software11 workplace examples
        Software to design or print custom cross-stitch or needlework patterns. May import photos or hand-drawn images.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborGraphics or photo imaging software225 workplace examples
        Software to draw or manipulate images. May adjust colors, add text, or apply special effects.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborVideo creation and editing software69 workplace examples
        Software to enable production of video content.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborWord processing software92 workplace examples
        Software to create, edit, store, or print documents.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborCharting software8 workplace examples
        Software to draw or create diagrams or charts. May include pre-built templates.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborPresentation software39 workplace examples
        Software to create presentations, speeches, or slideshows.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborWeb page creation and editing software63 workplace examples
        Software to develop or edit web sites or individual pages.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborCalendar and scheduling software87 workplace examples
        Software to plan or organize the activities of a person or group.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborSpreadsheet software15 workplace examples
        Software to create or edit numbers or text organized into rows and columns of cells. May include formulas which recalculate values based on other cells.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborOptical character reader OCR or scanning software27 workplace examples
        Software to convert an image of text, such as a scanned paper document or electronic fax file, into electronic text which can be edited or used in other applications.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborDesktop publishing software38 workplace examples
        Software to create or edit text and graphics into documents such as books, magazines, brochures, or manuals.
    • Folder (closedopen)Content management software
      Software to organize, store, or manage documents or media.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborContent workflow software13 workplace examples
        Software to create, manage, or optimize content creation processes. May include collaboration or scheduling features.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborDocument management software197 workplace examples
        Software to store, organize, or search documents or files from other software or from paper copies.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborFile versioning software7 workplace examples
        Software to archive and track edits to files for comparison and reference.
    • Folder (closedopen)Data management and query software
      Software to store, retrieve, or analyze data.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborCategorization or classification software16 workplace examples
        Software to organize or label large amounts of information for easier searching or management.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborClustering software6 workplace examples
        Software to combine and share resources across different technology tiers or architectures.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborCustomer relationship management CRM software140 workplace examples
        Software to manage customer interactions.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborData base management system software78 workplace examples
        Software to store, organize, or provide access to a set of related information.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborData base reporting software45 workplace examples
        Software to produce summaries or visualizations of raw data stored in a database system.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborData base user interface and query software910 workplace examples
        Software to query, present, or edit information stored in a database sytem.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborData mining software28 workplace examples
        Software to access or automatically extract information from a data source.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborInformation retrieval or search software117 workplace examples
        Software to efficiently search or access data from sources such as large collections of documents.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborMetadata management software28 workplace examples
        Software to manage or store "data about data" such as author, creation date, file size, or keywords.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborObject oriented data base management software8 workplace examples
        Software to store, organize, or provide access to data represented as a set of objects, rather than tables.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborPortal server software3 workplace examples
        Software to manage or create information portals.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborTransaction server software10 workplace examples
        Software to isolate business logic in programs from platform-specific code, so each can be maintained independently. May provide transaction management, clustering, failover, or load balancing.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborBusiness intelligence and data analysis software32 workplace examples
        Software to present, visualize, or analyze data to help businesses make informed decisions.
    • Folder (closedopen)Development software
      Software to design, build, test, or maintain applications.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborConfiguration management software26 workplace examples

        Software to create or maintain applications which run on a computer or mobile device.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborEnterprise application integration software31 workplace examples
        Software to connect different business systems to improve scalability or streamline work flows.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborGraphical user interface development software12 workplace examples
        Software to create or maintain applications which interact with users through a graphical interface.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborObject or component oriented development software68 workplace examples
        Software to create or maintain applications using object-oriented programming concepts.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborProgram testing software77 workplace examples
        Software to test or evaluate applications to ensure they comply with specified requirements.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborRequirements analysis and system architecture software12 workplace examples
        Software to create, manage, or monitor product development processes to comply with standards, specifications, or risk management policies.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborWeb platform development software73 workplace examples
        Software to create or maintain applications accessed through web sites or using web technologies such as HTML.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborCompiler and decompiler software22 workplace examples
        Software to transform code from one language to another. May convert human-readable source code into machine language.
