      Software to support learning or to access reference information.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborForeign language software16 workplace examples
        Software to translate text from one language to another, or to aid in learning or translating languages.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborComputer based training software126 workplace examples
        Software to create, manage, or provide instructional material or courses accessed from a computer.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborSpell checkers4 workplace examples
        Software to check the spelling of words in a document against a dictionary of preferred spellings.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborRoute navigation software20 workplace examples
        Software to assist users in moving from one location to another along a selected path or route.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborMulti-media educational software6 workplace examples
        Software to educate children or students using interactive or gaming elements.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborDictionary software3 workplace examples
        Software to provide the meaning and spelling of words. May include etymology or thesaurus features.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborVoice synthesizer and recognition software1 workplace example
        Software to generate or understand spoken words in order to provide alternative or easier-to-use application interfaces.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborGeographic information system37 workplace examples
        Software to capture, manage, analyze, or display geographically-referenced information.
    • Folder (closedopen)Industry specific software
      Software to support specific industries or specialized fields.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborAviation ground support software3 workplace examples
        Software to enable aircraft ground support operations. May include aircraft maintenence or baggage handling features.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborFacilities management software55 workplace examples
        Software to track, monitor, or analyze a facility's assets, space availability, or tenant information.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborComputer aided design CAD software344 workplace examples
        Software to design 2D or 3D objects. May include engineering or architectural features.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborAnalytical or scientific software1,687 workplace examples
        Software to capture data, perform analysis, or generate reports or visualizations to support scientific research.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborCompliance software125 workplace examples
        Software to monitor, check, or correct processes to achieve compliance with standards or federal, state, or local regulations.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborFlight control software8 workplace examples
        Software to identify and respond to changes in aircraft stability or control characteristics in order to maintain flight performance.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborIndustrial control software169 workplace examples
        Software to control, configure, or monitor industrial processes. May communicate with intelligent electronic devices.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborLibrary software37 workplace examples
        Software to acquire, catalog, search, or manage circulation of books or other library materials.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborMedical software671 workplace examples
        Software to evaluate, plan, or redesign healthcare procedures. May include reporting or visualization features.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborPoint of sale POS software104 workplace examples
        Software to enable billing and purchase of goods or services at retail markets or shops. May provide sales reports.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborComputer aided manufacturing CAM software123 workplace examples
        Software to control machine tools which fabricate or assemble objects.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborManufacturing execution system MES software1 workplace example
        Software to measure, control, or monitor production activities. May include material tracking, quality control, or scheduling features.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborComputer aided design CAD and computer aided manufacturing CAM system2 workplace examples
        Software to create and fabricate objects using computer-aided design (CAD) and computer-aided manufacturing (CAM) features.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborLegal management software3 workplace examples
        Software to enable operations of legal offices or courts. May include management of appointments, plantiffs or defendants, documents, or fees.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborRisk management data and analysis software10 workplace examples
        Software to collect, analyze, or visualize data to identify or assess risks.
    • Folder (closedopen)Network applications software
      Software to provide or access services over a network.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborApplication server software22 workplace examples
        Software to support or run applications which provide services through a communication protocol.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborDesktop communications software20 workplace examples
        Software to provide or interact with telephone system, address book, fax, or e-mail services.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborInteractive voice response software2 workplace examples
        Software to interact with telephone or voice mail callers using a pre-recorded database of voice messages, accepting input from voice or key presses.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborInternet directory services software9 workplace examples
        Software to create or manage the mapping of files, users, or other network resources to unique identifiers.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborInternet browser software12 workplace examples
        Software to view the contents of web pages and to navigate from one page to another.
    • Folder (closedopen)Network management software
      Software to monitor, control, or maintain computer networks.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborNetwork monitoring software75 workplace examples
        Software to monitor, analyze, or detect network systems.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborNetwork operating system enhancement software9 workplace examples
        Software to optimize the operations of OS software to maximize network speed or reliability.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborAdministration software23 workplace examples
        Software to manage computers, peripherals, or printers connected to a network. May detect new hardware or install software on connected devices.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborInternet protocol IP multimedia subsystem software10 workplace examples
        Software to enable services using the IP Multimedia Subsystem standard.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborCloud-based management software10 workplace examples
        Software to manage hybrid, private, or public cloud-based systems and infrastructure.
    • Folder (closedopen)Networking software
      Software to manage or support communication or resource sharing across networks.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborAccess software20 workplace examples
        Software to enable remote access of a computer system across a network.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborCommunications server software5 workplace examples
        Software to provide voice, fax, or data messaging services for an office or workgroup.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborContact center software3 workplace examples
        Software to manage customer interactions across multiple channels such as telephone, email, or social media.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborFax software1 workplace example
        Software to send, receive, or manage faxes.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborLAN software1 workplace example
        Software to enable communication across a local area network (LAN).
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborStorage networking software6 workplace examples
        Software to store or retrieve data on a network-attached device such as a hard drive or tape drive.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborSwitch or router software11 workplace examples
        Software to connect a local area network (LAN) to the Internet. May provide network address translation (NAT) or route traffic between LANs.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborWAN switching software and firmware3 workplace examples
        Software to enable communication across a wide-area network (WAN), which extends over a large geographic area.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborWireless software1 workplace example
        Software to enable communication over wireless network protocols.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborNetwork connectivity terminal emulation software4 workplace examples
        Software to communicate with remote host computers by imitating a hardware-based network terminal such as a VT-100.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborGateway software2 workplace examples
        Software to enable data flow between two networks.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborBridge software1 workplace example
        Software to forward traffic between network segments, based on data link layer information.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborPlatform interconnectivity software6 workplace examples
        Software to enable communication between computers running different operating systems.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborSoftware defined networking/ virtualization software1 workplace example
        Software to control or direct network traffic using virtual networks which work in conjunction with a physical network.
    • Folder (closedopen)Operating environment software
      Software to run or manage computer systems or support applications.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborFilesystem software13 workplace examples
        Software to manage or create a file system for a computing device.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborNetwork operation system software3 workplace examples
        Software to connect computers or devices to a network by providing or enhancing the base operating system.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborOperating system software53 workplace examples
        Software to provide the operational interface to a computer and provide higher-level services and APIs.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborComputer imaging software1 workplace example
        Software to create, manage, or apply images of a computer's operating system and files, to configure or restore computers to a known state.
    • Folder (closedopen)Security and protection software
      Software to protect systems or data from unauthorized access or threats.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborAuthentication server software6 workplace examples
        Software to provide services for authenticating users across a network.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborNetwork security or virtual private network VPN management software26 workplace examples
        Software to manage network access capabilities or policies.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborNetwork security and virtual private network VPN equipment software14 workplace examples
        Software to configure or maintain security or virtual private network (VPN) equipment.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborTransaction security and virus protection software43 workplace examples
        Software to protect sensitive information, ensure the safe transfer of data, or detect or remove malware or computer viruses.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborCloud-based protection or security software3 workplace examples
        Software to protect information and resources in cloud environments, or to protect systems by using a cloud-based service for updates or configuration.
    • Folder (closedopen)Utility and device driver software
      Software to maintain system performance or to enable hardware devices to function properly.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborData conversion software8 workplace examples
        Software to transform information from one format to another.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborData compression software3 workplace examples
        Software to encode data in order to reduce storage space or network transmission time.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborDevice drivers or system software12 workplace examples
        Software to enable communication between hardware devices and applications or operating systems.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborVoice recognition software15 workplace examples
        Software to convert spoken words into electronic text.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborStorage media loading software2 workplace examples
        Software to control devices which automatically load or store data on media such as tapes, discs, or drives.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborBackup or archival software25 workplace examples
        Software to back up information from computer systems to additional destinations such as networked servers or tape drives.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborText to speech conversion software1 workplace example
        Software to convert electronic text into spoken words.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborEye tracking software3 workplace examples
        Software to track eye movements via a camera or similar device. May emulate a computer mouse or other input device.
    • Folder (closedopen)Information exchange software
      Software to send, receive, or manage information between systems or users.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborElectronic mail software20 workplace examples
        Software to compose, send, or receive email.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborVideo conferencing software15 workplace examples
        Software to enable audio or video conferences between participants using networked devices.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborNetwork conferencing software12 workplace examples
        Software to share files, remotely control computers, or transmit real-time data between participants using networked devices.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborInstant messaging software7 workplace examples
        Software to enable private communication between participants using networked devices. May include chat rooms or notification services.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborMap creation software111 workplace examples
        Software to place data or metadata on a map. May include surveying or engineering features.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborMobile operator specific application software4 workplace examples
        Software to support operations of mobile network or telecommunications service providers. May include billing, network planning, or customer service features.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborMobile messaging service software2 workplace examples
        Software to send or receive text or voice messages on a mobile device. May include SMS or voice mail capabilities.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborMobile location based services software23 workplace examples
        Software to provide content or services based on a device's geographical location.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborCloud-based data access and sharing software10 workplace examples
        Software to share or co-author computer files through cloud computing, where documents are stored in a central "cloud" of networked servers.
    • Folder (closedopen)System management software
      Software to monitor, manage, or maintain IT systems, applications, and infrastructure.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborEnterprise system management software6 workplace examples
        Software to manage activities across enterprise IT systems, such as evaluating capacity or server availability, monitoring user activities, or installing software.
  • Folder (closedopen) Essential Skills10 variables 17,880 ratings
    Skills you need for most jobs such as reading, writing, math, and learning.
    • Folder (closedopen)Foundational Areas
      Areas that support learning and applying skills across many types of work.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborReading Comprehension
        Understanding written sentences and paragraphs in work-related documents.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborActive Listening
        Giving full attention to what other people are saying, taking time to understand the points being made, asking questions as appropriate, and not interrupting at inappropriate times.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborWriting
        Communicating effectively in writing as appropriate for the needs of the audience.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborSpeaking
        Talking to others to convey information effectively.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborMathematics
        Using mathematics to solve problems.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborScience
        Using scientific rules and methods to solve problems.
    • Folder (closedopen)Ways of Working
      Ways of doing work that support learning and building skills.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborCritical Thinking
        Using logic and reasoning to identify the strengths and weaknesses of alternative solutions, conclusions, or approaches to problems.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborActive Learning
        Understanding the implications of new information for both current and future problem-solving and decision-making.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborLearning Strategies
        Selecting and using training/instructional methods and procedures appropriate for the situation when learning or teaching new things.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborMonitoring
        Monitoring/Assessing performance of yourself, other individuals, or organizations to make improvements or take corrective action.
  • Folder (closedopen) Transferable Skills25 variables 44,700 ratings
    Skills you can use across different jobs such as communication, problem solving, and team work.
    • Folder (closedopen)Social Skills
      Skills used to work with others to achieve goals at work.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborSocial Perceptiveness
        Being aware of others' reactions and understanding why they react as they do.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborCoordination
        Adjusting actions in relation to others' actions.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborPersuasion
        Persuading others to change their minds or behavior.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborNegotiation
        Bringing others together and trying to reconcile differences.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborInstructing
        Teaching others how to do something.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborService Orientation
        Actively looking for ways to help people.
    • Folder (closedopen)Complex Problem Solving Skills
      Skills used to solve new and challenging problems at work.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborComplex Problem Solving
        Identifying complex problems and reviewing related information to develop and evaluate options and implement solutions.
    • Folder (closedopen)Technical Skills
      Skills used to design, set up, operate, and fix machines and systems at work.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborOperations Analysis
        Analyzing needs and product requirements to create a design.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborTechnology Design
        Generating or adapting equipment and technology to serve user needs.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborEquipment Selection
        Determining the kind of tools and equipment needed to do a job.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborInstallation
        Installing equipment, machines, wiring, or programs to meet specifications.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborProgramming
        Writing computer programs for various purposes.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborOperations Monitoring
        Watching gauges, dials, or other indicators to make sure a machine is working properly.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborOperation and Control
        Controlling operations of equipment or systems.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborEquipment Maintenance
        Performing routine maintenance on equipment and determining when and what kind of maintenance is needed.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborTroubleshooting
        Determining causes of operating errors and deciding what to do about it.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborRepairing
        Repairing machines or systems using the needed tools.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborQuality Control Analysis
        Conducting tests and inspections of products, services, or processes to evaluate quality or performance.
    • Folder (closedopen)Systems Skills
      Skills used to understand and improve systems at work, such as processes, decision making, or workflows.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborJudgment and Decision Making
        Considering the relative costs and benefits of potential actions to choose the most appropriate one.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborSystems Analysis
        Determining how a system should work and how changes in conditions, operations, and the environment will affect outcomes.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborSystems Evaluation
        Identifying measures or indicators of system performance and the actions needed to improve or correct performance, relative to the goals of the system.
    • Folder (closedopen)Resource Management Skills
      Skills used to manage time, money, materials, and people effectively at work.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborTime Management
        Managing one's own time and the time of others.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborManagement of Financial Resources
        Determining how money will be spent to get the work done, and accounting for these expenditures.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborManagement of Material Resources
        Obtaining and seeing to the appropriate use of equipment, facilities, and materials needed to do certain work.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborManagement of Personnel Resources
        Motivating, developing, and directing people as they work, identifying the best people for the job.
  • Folder (closedopen) Knowledge33 variables 59,004 ratings
    Information and understanding used across different areas of work.
    • Folder (closedopen)Business and Management
      Knowledge of principles and facts related to business administration and accounting, human and material resource management in organizations, sales and marketing, economics, and office information and organizing systems
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborAdministration and Management
        Knowledge of business and management principles involved in strategic planning, resource allocation, human resources modeling, leadership technique, production methods, and coordination of people and resources.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborAdministrative
        Knowledge of administrative and office procedures and systems such as word processing, managing files and records, stenography and transcription, designing forms, and workplace terminology.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborEconomics and Accounting
        Knowledge of economic and accounting principles and practices, the financial markets, banking, and the analysis and reporting of financial data.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborSales and Marketing
        Knowledge of principles and methods for showing, promoting, and selling products or services. This includes marketing strategy and tactics, product demonstration, sales techniques, and sales control systems.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborCustomer and Personal Service
        Knowledge of principles and processes for providing customer and personal services. This includes customer needs assessment, meeting quality standards for services, and evaluation of customer satisfaction.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborPersonnel and Human Resources
        Knowledge of principles and procedures for personnel recruitment, selection, training, compensation and benefits, labor relations and negotiation, and personnel information systems.
    • Folder (closedopen)Manufacturing and Production
      Knowledge of principles and facts related to the production, processing, storage, and distribution of manufactured and agricultural goods
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborProduction and Processing
        Knowledge of raw materials, production processes, quality control, costs, and other techniques for maximizing the effective manufacture and distribution of goods.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborFood Production
        Knowledge of techniques and equipment for planting, growing, and harvesting food products (both plant and animal) for consumption, including storage/handling techniques.
    • Folder (closedopen)Engineering and Technology
      Knowledge of the design, development, and application of technology for specific purposes.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborComputers and Electronics
        Knowledge of circuit boards, processors, chips, electronic equipment, and computer hardware and software, including applications and programming.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborEngineering and Technology
        Knowledge of the practical application of engineering science and technology. This includes applying principles, techniques, procedures, and equipment to the design and production of various goods and services.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborDesign
        Knowledge of design techniques, tools, and principles involved in production of precision technical plans, blueprints, drawings, and models.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborBuilding and Construction
        Knowledge of materials, methods, and the tools involved in the construction or repair of houses, buildings, or other structures such as highways and roads.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborMechanical
        Knowledge of machines and tools, including their designs, uses, repair, and maintenance.
    • Folder (closedopen)Mathematics and Science
      Knowledge of the history, theories, methods, and applications of the physical, biological, social, mathematical, and geography
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborMathematics
        Knowledge of arithmetic, algebra, geometry, calculus, statistics, and their applications.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborPhysics
        Knowledge and prediction of physical principles, laws, their interrelationships, and applications to understanding fluid, material, and atmospheric dynamics, and mechanical, electrical, atomic and sub-atomic structures and processes.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborChemistry
        Knowledge of the chemical composition, structure, and properties of substances and of the chemical processes and transformations that they undergo. This includes uses of chemicals and their interactions, danger signs, production techniques, and disposal methods.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborBiology
        Knowledge of plant and animal organisms, their tissues, cells, functions, interdependencies, and interactions with each other and the environment.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborPsychology
        Knowledge of human behavior and performance; individual differences in ability, personality, and interests; learning and motivation; psychological research methods; and the assessment and treatment of behavioral and affective disorders.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborSociology and Anthropology
        Knowledge of group behavior and dynamics, societal trends and influences, human migrations, ethnicity, cultures, and their history and origins.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborGeography
        Knowledge of principles and methods for describing the features of land, sea, and air masses, including their physical characteristics, locations, interrelationships, and distribution of plant, animal, and human life.
    • Folder (closedopen)Health Services
      Knowledge of principles and facts regarding diagnosing, curing, and preventing disease, and improving and preserving physical and mental health and well-being
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborMedicine and Dentistry
        Knowledge of the information and techniques needed to diagnose and treat human injuries, diseases, and deformities. This includes symptoms, treatment alternatives, drug properties and interactions, and preventive health-care measures.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborTherapy and Counseling
        Knowledge of principles, methods, and procedures for diagnosis, treatment, and rehabilitation of physical and mental dysfunctions, and for career counseling and guidance.
    • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborEducation and Training
      Knowledge of principles and methods for curriculum and training design, teaching and instruction for individuals and groups, and the measurement of training effects.
    • Folder (closedopen)Arts and Humanities
      Knowledge of facts and principles related to the branches of learning concerned with human thought, language, and the arts.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborEnglish Language
        Knowledge of the structure and content of the English language including the meaning and spelling of words, and rules of composition and grammar.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborForeign Language
        Knowledge of the structure and content of a foreign (non-English) language including the meaning and spelling of words, rules of composition and grammar, and pronunciation.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborFine Arts
        Knowledge of the theory and techniques required to compose, produce, and perform works of music, dance, visual arts, drama, and sculpture.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborHistory and Archeology
        Knowledge of historical events and their causes, indicators, and effects on civilizations and cultures.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborPhilosophy and Theology
        Knowledge of different philosophical systems and religions. This includes their basic principles, values, ethics, ways of thinking, customs, practices, and their impact on human culture.
    • Folder (closedopen)Law and Public Safety
      Knowledge of regulations and methods for maintaining people and property free from danger, injury, or damage; the rules of public conduct established and enforced by legislation, and the political process establishing such rules.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborPublic Safety and Security
        Knowledge of relevant equipment, policies, procedures, and strategies to promote effective local, state, or national security operations for the protection of people, data, property, and institutions.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborLaw and Government
        Knowledge of laws, legal codes, court procedures, precedents, government regulations, executive orders, agency rules, and the democratic political process.
    • Folder (closedopen)Communications
      Knowledge of the science and art of delivering information
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborTelecommunications
        Knowledge of transmission, broadcasting, switching, control, and operation of telecommunications systems.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborCommunications and Media
        Knowledge of media production, communication, and dissemination techniques and methods. This includes alternative ways to inform and entertain via written, oral, and visual media.
    • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborTransportation
      Knowledge of principles and methods for moving people or goods by air, rail, sea, or road, including the relative costs and benefits.
  • Folder (closedopen) Education2 variables 11,100 ratings
    Education typically required to perform a job.
    • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborRequired Level of Education
      The level of education required to perform a job.
    • Folder (closedopen)Job-Related Professional Certification
      Certification: A credential awarded by a certification body based on an individual demonstrating through an examination process that he or she has acquired the designated knowledge, skills, and abilities to perform a specific job.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborJob-Related Professional Certification
        Possession of an occupational or industry certification to perform the job.
    • Data collected by Classification of Instructional Programs, U.S. Department of EducationInstructional Program Required
      The instructional program required for this job
    • Folder (closedopen)Education Level in Specific Subjects
      The amount of education required in 15 subject areas to perform in a job. Subject areas cover most of the courses that occur in high school, junior college, college undergraduate degree programs, and other education and training programs
      • Data not collectedTechnical Vocational
        Courses focus on non-business technical skills, such as Agriculture, Industrial Arts, Automobile and Shop, and Electronics
      • Data not collectedBusiness Vocational
        Courses focus on basic business skills, such as Word Processing, Filing, Bookkeeping/Basic Accounting
      • Data not collectedEnglish/language Arts
        Courses focus on reading, interpretation, and writing, such as Literature, Composition, Journalism, and Creative Writing
      • Data not collectedOral Communication
        Courses focus on oral communication and speech, such as Oral Communication, Speech, and Interpersonal Communication
      • Data not collectedLanguages
        Courses focus on reading, writing, and/or speaking languages other than English, such as French, Chinese, German, Japanese, Latin, Russian, and Spanish
      • Data not collectedBasic Math
        Courses focus on basic and applied math, such as General Math and Business Math
      • Data not collectedAdvanced Math
        Courses focus on advanced topics in math, such as Algebra, Geometry, Calculus, and Statistics
      • Data not collectedPhysical Science
        Courses focus on the study of matter and/or energy, such as Physics, Chemistry, and Astronomy
      • Data not collectedComputer Science
        Courses focus on computers and their uses, such as Programming, Information Systems Management, and Software Applications
      • Data not collectedBiological Science
        Courses focus on the study of life and living beings, such as life science, biology, anatomy and physiology
      • Data not collectedApplied Science
        Courses focus on the application of science, such as Engineering, Health, and Medicine
      • Data not collectedSocial Science
        Courses focus on the behavioral sciences, such as Social Studies, Economics, History, Psychology, and Sociology
      • Data not collectedArts
        Courses focus on visual and performing arts, such as Arts and Crafts, Music, Painting, Sculpture, Theater, and Voice
      • Data not collectedHumanities
        Courses focus on cultural and philosophical aspects of humans, such as Minority Studies, Philosophy, and Religion
      • Data not collectedPhysical Education
        Courses focus on physical fitness and sports, such as Aerobics, Jogging, Weight Lifting, and Specific Sports

Experience Requirements

Work experience, training, or certifications needed for certain jobs.

  • Folder (closedopen) Experience and Training4 variables 26,025 ratings
    The amount of related work experience and training needed for a job.
    • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborRelated Work Experience
      Amount of related work experience required to get hired for the job?
    • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborOn-Site or In-Plant Training
      Amount of on-site or in-plant training (e.g., organized class room instruction) required to perform the job?
    • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborOn-the-Job Training
      Amount of on the job training required to perform the job?
    • Folder (closedopen)Apprenticeship
      Apprenticeship is a combination of on-the-job training and related instruction in which workers learn the practical and theoretical aspects of a highly skilled occupation. Apprenticeship programs can be sponsored by individual employers, joint employer and labor groups, and/or employer associations.
      • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborJob-related Apprenticeship
        Completion of a job-related apprenticeship to perform the job.
  • Folder (closedopen) Licensing
    Required licenses, certifications, or registrations needed to perform a job.
    • Data collected by CareerOneStop, U.S. Department of LaborLicense, Certificate, or Registration Required
      At least one license, certificate, or registration is required to perform in this job, including a driver's or vehicle operator's license. The specific license(s), certificate(s), or registration(s) are listed.
    • Folder (closedopen)Specific License or Certificate Required
      Specific education, training, examination, or other requirements for obtaining the licenses, certificates, or registration needed to perform in this job
      • Data not collectedPost-Secondary Degree
        Obtaining the licenses, certificates, or registration needed to perform in this job requires a post-secondary degree, for example an Associate's or Bachelor's degree.
      • Data not collectedGraduate Degree
        Obtaining the licenses, certificates, or registration needed to perform in this job requires a graduate degree, for example, a Master's or Doctoral degree.
      • Data not collectedOn-the-Job Training
        Obtaining the licenses, certificates, or registration needed to perform in this job requires on-the-job training, including apprenticeships, internships, and other supervised experiences.
      • Data not collectedExamination
        Obtaining the licenses, certificates, or registration needed to perform in this job requires an examination, for example, written, oral, or performance assessments.
      • Data not collectedCharacter References
        Obtaining the licenses, certificates, or registration needed to perform in this job requires one or more character references from other individuals.
    • Data collected by CareerOneStop, U.S. Department of LaborAdditional Education and Training
      Retaining the licenses, certificates, or registration needed to perform in this job requires additional course work.
    • Folder (closedopen)Organization and Agency Requirements
      Organizations or agencies requiring the specific licenses, certificates, or registration needed to perform in a job
      • Data not collectedLegal Requirement
        Federal, state, or local law requires possessing specific licenses, certificates, or registration for performance in this job.
      • Data not collectedEmployer Requirement
        Employers require possessing specific licenses, certificates, or registration for performance in this job.
      • Data not collectedUnion, Guild, or Professional Association
        A union or professional association requires possessing specific licenses, certificates, or registration for performance in this job.

Occupational Requirements

What jobs involve, including work activities, conditions, and context.

  • Folder (closedopen) Work Activities41 variables 73,308 ratings 2,083 detailed work activities 23,850 DWA-task linkages
    Activities performed across a number of occupations. This hierarchy includes generalized, intermediate, and detailed work activities.
    • Folder (closedopen)Information Input
      Where and how are the information and data gained that are needed to perform this job?
      • Folder (closedopen)Looking for and Receiving Job-Related Information
        How is information obtained to perform this job?
        • Folder (closedopen)Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborGetting Information23 intermediate work activities 111 detailed work activities
          Observing, receiving, and otherwise obtaining information from all relevant sources.
          • Folder (closedopen)Study details of artistic productions.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborReview art or design materials.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborStudy details of musical compositions.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborReview production information to determine costume or makeup requirements.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborStudy scripts to determine project requirements.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborReview audio or video recordings.
          • Folder (closedopen)Read documents or materials to inform work processes.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborRead materials to determine needed actions.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborRead maps to determine routes.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborReview customer information.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborRead work orders or other instructions to determine product specifications or materials requirements.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborRead technical information needed to perform maintenance or repairs.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborStudy blueprints or other instructions to determine equipment setup requirements.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborRead documents to gather technical information.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborReview technical documents to plan work.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborReview blueprints or specifications to determine work requirements.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborReview work orders or schedules to determine operations or procedures.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborReview blueprints or other instructions to determine operational methods or sequences.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborEvaluate reports or designs to determine work needs.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborRead work orders or descriptions of problems to determine repairs or modifications needed.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborInterpret blueprints, specifications, or diagrams to inform installation, development or operation activities.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborReview details of technical drawings or specifications.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborRead work orders to determine material or setup requirements.
          • Folder (closedopen)Investigate criminal or legal matters.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborCollect evidence for legal proceedings.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborExamine crime scenes to obtain evidence.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborInvestigate crimes committed within organizations.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborInvestigate legal issues.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborExamine records or other types of data to investigate criminal activities.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborConduct hearings to investigate legal issues.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborInvestigate illegal or suspicious activities.
          • Folder (closedopen)Gather information from physical or electronic sources.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborObtain property information.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborUse databases to locate investigation details or other information.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborSearch information sources to find specific data.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborGather financial records.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborRetrieve information from electronic sources.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborGather information in order to provide services to clients.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborSearch files, databases or reference materials to obtain needed information.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborCollect archival data.
          • Folder (closedopen)Consult legal materials or public records.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborEvaluate information related to legal matters in public or personal records.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborResearch relevant legal materials to aid decision making.
          • Folder (closedopen)Gather data about operational or development activities.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborGather organizational performance information.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborGather information about work conditions or locations.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborGather physical survey data.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborCollect data about project sites.
          • Folder (closedopen)Obtain information about goods or services.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborObtain information about goods or services.
          • Folder (closedopen)Research issues related to earth sciences.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborResearch hydrologic features or processes.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborResearch geological features or processes.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborConduct climatological research.
          • Folder (closedopen)Research organizational behavior, processes, or performance.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborConduct surveys in organizations.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborResearch industrial processes or operations.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborConduct scientific research of organizational behavior or processes.
          • Folder (closedopen)Investigate the environmental impact of industrial or development activities.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborResearch impacts of environmental conservation initiatives.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborResearch issues related to the environment or sustainable business practices.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborInvestigate the environmental impact of projects.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborMonitor environmental impacts of production or development activities.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborResearch environmental impact of industrial or development activities.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborIdentify environmental concerns.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborEvaluate environmental impact of operational or development activities.
          • Folder (closedopen)Gather information for news stories.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborGather information for news stories.
          • Folder (closedopen)Collect information about patients or clients.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborGather medical information from patient histories.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborCollect medical information from patients, family members, or other medical professionals.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborCollect information from people through observation, interviews, or surveys.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborCollect information about clients.
          • Folder (closedopen)Research agricultural processes or practices.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborResearch livestock management methods.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborResearch crop management methods.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborResearch sustainable agricultural processes or practices.
          • Folder (closedopen)Collect data about consumer needs or opinions.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborConduct market research.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborGather customer or product information to determine customer needs.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborObtain personal or financial information about customers or applicants.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborConduct opinion surveys or needs assessments.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborCollect data about customer needs.
          • Folder (closedopen)Investigate individuals' background, behavior, or activities.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborObserve individuals' activities to gather information or compile evidence.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborInvestigate personal characteristics or activities of individuals.
          • Folder (closedopen)Research biological or ecological phenomena.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborResearch genetic characteristics or expression.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborResearch diseases or parasites.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborConduct research of processes in natural or industrial ecosystems.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborResearch microbiological or chemical processes or structures.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborClassify organisms based on their characteristics or behavior.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborExamine characteristics or behavior of living organisms.
          • Folder (closedopen)Obtain formal documentation or authorization.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborObtain documentation to authorize activities.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborObtain copyrights or other legal permissions.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborObtain written authorization to perform activities.
          • Folder (closedopen)Research historical or social issues.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborConduct anthropological or archaeological research.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborConduct historical research.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborConduct research on social issues.
          • Folder (closedopen)Research healthcare issues.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborConduct research to increase knowledge about medical issues.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborCollect information about community health needs.
          • Folder (closedopen)Research technology designs or applications.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborResearch engineering aspects of biological or chemical processes.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborResearch engineering applications of emerging technologies.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborResearch design or application of green technologies.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborConduct research to gain information about products or processes.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborResearch advanced engineering designs or applications.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborResearch energy production, use, or conservation.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborResearch human performance or health factors related to engineering or design activities.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborResearch new technologies.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborResearch product safety.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborResearch methods to improve food products.
          • Folder (closedopen)Investigate incidents or accidents.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborInvestigate accidents to determine causes.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborExamine debris to obtain information about causes of fires.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborInvestigate transportation incidents, violations, or complaints.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborInvestigate industrial or transportation accidents.
          • Folder (closedopen)Investigate organizational or operational problems.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborInvestigate system, equipment, or product failures.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborInvestigate work related complaints to determine corrective actions.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborInvestigate safety of work environment.
          • Folder (closedopen)Interview people to obtain information.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborInterview employees, customers, or others to collect information.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborInterview others for news or entertainment purposes.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborInterview clients to gather financial information.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborInterview people to obtain information about actions or status of individuals.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborInterview people to gather information about criminal activities.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborInterview patients to gather medical information.
            • Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborInterview claimants to get information related to legal proceedings.

    • Folder (closedopen)Work Output
      What physical activities are performed, what equipment and vehicles are operated/controlled, and what complex/technical activities are accomplished as job outputs?
      • Folder (closedopen)Performing Physical and Manual Work Activities
        What activities using the body and hands are done to perform this job?
        • Folder (closedopen)Data collected by O*NET Data Collection Program, U.S. Department of LaborPerforming General Physical Activities17 intermediate work activities 126 detailed work activities
